hypothesis: Incorporating explicit many-body dispersion interactions (beyond pairwise DFT-D3 corrections) into the training data generation pipeline for uMLIPs—specifically by computing reference energies and forces at the PBE+MBD level rather than PBE+D3(BJ)—will disproportionately improve uMLIP accuracy for MOF properties that depend on long-range collective polarization effects (such as guest-free heat capacity and bulk modulus of large-pore, low-density frameworks), while providing negligible benefit for dense, high-metal-node-density MOFs where pairwise dispersion already captures the dominant interactions.
